Module 1 · NotebookLM Advanced
What Is NotebookLM?
Click each card to compare — flip to see the difference
🧠
NotebookLM
Click to reveal
Your AI, trained on YOUR files
Only uses what you upload. Perfect for personalised teaching materials.
💬
ChatGPT
Click to reveal
General AI, trained on the internet
Broad knowledge. Great for generating new content. May hallucinate on specific local context.
Today with NLM:   ① Extract content  ·  ② Set personas  ·  ③ Generate IP slides
Module 1 · NotebookLM Advanced
What Is Data Extraction?
The idea is simple:
📂 ➜ 💬 ➜ ✨
Drop your messy teaching materials in, then ask NLM to pull out exactly what you need — in any format.
📋
Lists
📊
Tables
🖼️
Infographics
Why is this powerful?
Instead of spending hours reformatting notes, you just ask. NLM reads your file and gives you a structured output instantly.
Module 1 · Try It #1
Generate an Infographic
Demo first:
1
Upload your material
Drag any teaching document into NLM
2
Ask in the chat
"Generate an infographic summarising this"
3
Get structured output
Download or screenshot the result

Create your own infographic

② Extract → generate an infographic

Example roles: Chinese question generator · homeroom letter writer · lesson planner · parent reply assistant

Module 1 · NotebookLM Advanced
Your Personal
IP Character
Turn your teaching style and image into an AI-callable character that appears on every slide of your presentation.
Module 1 · Demo
Generate Your IP Character
1
Upload your photo
A clear, front-facing photo works best
2
Add a style prompt
e.g. "Transform into a friendly cartoon educator in warm colours"
3
Download your IP image
PNG format recommended for transparency
4
Save it — you'll use it next
This becomes your signature teaching avatar
Why it matters
Every slide of your AI-generated presentation features your character — making it instantly recognisable as yours. Students connect with it too.
IP Character Generator ↗
Module 1 · NotebookLM Advanced
Slide Structure Generator
1
Extract content → generate outline
In the NLM chat: "Generate a 10-slide outline for this topic"
2
Download as .txt
Copy the outline → save as a .txt file on your device
3
Re-upload to NLM as a new source
Now NLM has your structure as a separate reference
4
Select .txt + IP image → Generate Slides
Tick both sources → click Generate Presentation
Slide Generator ↗
Module 1 · Try It #3
Generate Your Presentation

Generate outline in chat
2
Save as .txt → re-upload
3
Select .txt + your IP image
4
Generate Slides → done

① Combine your IP image + outline

Module 1 · Gemini Integration
NotebookLM × Gemini
Your notebook just got a brain upgrade. 4 new powers — all built into the sidebar you already know.
🧠
Contextual Memory
AI remembers everything you said this session
🎭
Custom Persona
Switch expert roles without re-explaining context
🎨
Gemini Ecosystem
Text → posters, charts, Word docs in one place
🎧
Chat as a Source
Gemini conversations become free NLM sources
Module 1 · Gemini Power 1 of 4
🧠 Contextual Memory
Your AI remembers everything you said this session.
Before Gemini, each question in NLM was a fresh start. Now Gemini follows your train of thought and builds on every step you've taken.
Core advantage
No more copy-pasting background context. Your AI works like a real teaching assistant — pushing your lesson design deeper, prompt by prompt.
Example: Upload a Chinese lesson unit
1
"Design a 40-minute lesson plan for this unit."
2
"Change the second activity into a practice exercise for lower-ability students."
3
"Based on that exercise, write a short message to parents about tonight's parent-child reading activity."
Three prompts. One context. Lesson plan → differentiation → parent message — no re-explaining needed.
Module 1 · Gemini Power 2 of 4
🎭 Custom Persona
Same materials. Different expert. One switch.
Set a persona for your notebook — Gemini responds in exactly that role, tone, and level of language every time.
Core advantage
The same batch of lesson materials can generate beginner-friendly explanations in the morning and exam-level questions in the afternoon.
Example: Same Chinese lesson, two personas
🟢 Lower Primary Reading Guide
"You are a warm reading guide for Primary 2 students. Explain each vocabulary word simply, give one example sentence, and add a friendly emoji."
→ Casual tone · short sentences · emoji-friendly
🔵 PSLE Question Setter
"You are a strict PSLE-level question setter. Generate 5 comprehension questions that require inference — no straightforward recall."
→ Formal · exam-standard · high-order thinking
Module 1 · Gemini Power 3 of 4
🎨 Gemini Ecosystem
From text to visual — without leaving the sidebar.
Before Gemini, NLM only output plain text. Now you can go directly from notebook content to posters, charts, and documents — all in one place.
Core advantage
No more copy-pasting between tools. From "read the text" to "publish the poster" is one workflow, not three separate apps.
Example: Upload a science experiment text
🖼️
Visual Teaching Poster
"Turn the experiment steps in Chapter 3 into a 4-panel comic-style teaching poster."
📊
Data Chart for Assessment
"Generate a bar chart from the comparison data in this text — ready to paste into an exam paper."
Module 1 · Gemini Power 4 of 4
🎧 Chat as a Source
Your Gemini conversation becomes an NLM source — for free.
Every Gemini chat saved in your notebook becomes an independent source. It does not count toward your 50-document limit.
Core advantage
Tick that conversation source when generating an Audio Overview — and your podcast becomes a real dialogue, not a textbook recitation.
Example: Chinese lesson 《The Lost Golden Key》
1
Start a Gemini debate: "Should the main character return the key?"
2
The conversation is saved as a source automatically
3
Tick the conversation source → Generate Audio Overview
4
Send to class group as a listening & extension activity
⚠️ Watch Out
Don't Mix Public Sharing with Private Chat
If you plan to use Gemini for deep lesson planning — do not share that notebook's link with other teachers. The moment you make it public, it disappears from your Gemini sidebar.
✅ Private Notebook + Gemini Chat
Your personal lesson planning, differentiation, and question generation.
Gemini can see it. You iterate freely.
📤 Shared Notebook (public link)
Copy a clean notebook → share the link with colleagues.
Keep this completely separate from your working copy.

GEMINI
Gem
Your personal AI assistant — pre-configured, always ready
Gemini Gem
What Is a Gem?
Unlike a regular chat, a Gem remembers its role every time you open it.
You set it up once — it stays focused on exactly what you need, every session.
Think of it as a specialist assistant who never forgets their job.
📚
Teaching
Quiz generator · Lesson planner · Vocabulary coach
🌱
Personal
Reflection coach · Goal tracker · Daily journaling
📋
Admin
Email drafter · Meeting notes · Report helper
Gemini Gem · Design
Think First. Then Build.
💭 Which area of your life needs an AI assistant most?
📚 A teaching task you repeat every week
🌱 A personal habit or growth goal
📋 An admin job that drains your time
Take 1 minute to decide before you start building.
Build your Gem in 3 steps
1
Open the Gem Designer link below
2
Answer 5 guided questions — AI writes your System Instructions
3
Gemini → New Gem → paste instructions → name it → Save
💎 Open Gem Designer ↗
Gemini Gem · Try It
Build Your Personal Gem
Follow the steps
1
Open Gem Designer → answer 5 questions
2
Copy the System Instructions it generates
3
Gemini → New Gem → paste → name it → Save
4
Test it — ask your Gem one real question
🎯 Your Turn — Padlet #6
Share your Gem with everyone
① Share your Gem link to Padlet
② Caption: Gem name + what it does
③ Try someone else's Gem 📸
⏱ 15 minutes
Bonus Module
Chrome
Built-in AI
Chrome Skill
Ask Gemini Chrome Skills Cross-tab AI
Chrome Built-in AI · Ask Gemini
What Is Chrome Built-in Gemini?
An AI sidebar built directly into Chrome. Ask questions, organise content and generate images — without switching tabs.
✅ 3 Steps to Enable
1
Update Chrome to the latest version and relaunch
2
Settings → AI Innovation Features → Chrome Built-in Gemini → Turn On
3
Settings → AI Innovation Features → Manage Linked Apps → Confirm Google Workspace is ON
📍 Where to Find It
Click the ✨ star icon in the top-right of Chrome, or use the floating menu that appears when you select text
💡 What Can It Do?
Ask questions · Summarise the current page
Connect to Gmail / Maps / YouTube
Organise across tabs · Generate images
Chrome Built-in AI · Try It
Four Live Demos
📧
Gmail Unread
Link Google Gmail
Ask: "Show me my unread emails"
🗺️
Google Maps
Ask: "What are famous landmarks near Bandar Seri Begawan?"
▶️
YouTube History
Link YouTube
Ask: "How many videos have I watched?"
🖼️
Redesign Image
Upload a photo
Ask: "Redesign this image in 1:1 ratio"
Chrome Built-in AI · Cross-tab Feature
Organise Multiple Tabs into a Table
Chrome's built-in Gemini can read multiple open tabs at once and compile the content — no copying needed.
📋 Demo Steps
1
Open several product pages (different brands / prices)
2
Open the Gemini sidebar · Use @ to select all product tabs
3
Type your prompt → Gemini generates a comparison table
"Compare these massage gun pages — features, price. Put it in a table."
🏫 Classroom Applications
Compare multiple learning websites
Compile highlights from reference articles
Summarise students' work across tabs
✨ Key Advantage
No tab-switching · No copy-pasting
One prompt, cross-tab intelligence
Chrome Built-in AI · Chrome Skills
Chrome Skills — Zero-Input Automation
Turn AI into a native browser button. Package your most-used prompts and run them with a single click ⚡
🔧 How to Enable
1
In Chrome, go to chrome://flags/#skills
2
Find Skills at the top of the page → Change from Default to Enabled
3
Click Relaunch at the bottom of the page
Three Advantages
🏠
Native
Runs inside the browser — no tab switching needed
🔒
Private
Safer than third-party extensions
📚
Personal
Build your own skill library tailored to your workflow
Chrome Built-in AI · Must-Know Shortcuts
Two Power Symbols + Build Your Own Skill
Power Symbols
@
Data Symbol
Specify your data source
e.g. @ current page · @ a specific tab
/
Action Symbol
Specify the action to run
e.g. / generate image · / data viz · / summarise
Build Your Own Skill ⚡
Type a prompt you use often
(e.g. design a worksheet · generate a lesson plan · summarise key points)
Click the ⚡ lightning icon next to the prompt
Name it → saved permanently as your own Skill
🎯 What Will Your Skill Do?
Lesson prep · Parent messages · Marking feedback
Meeting notes · Personal growth journaling
Chrome Built-in AI · Your Skill Toolkit
6 Teaching Skills — Click to Copy
Copy any prompt → Paste into Chrome AI sidebar → Click ⚡ to save as your Skill
📋
001 · Key Summary
Condense any content into clear, structured key points
📝
002 · Quiz Generator
Generate 5 MCQs with answers and explanations
📄
003 · Worksheet Design
Design a ready-to-use student worksheet
🎨
004 · AI Image Prompt
Write a detailed prompt for AI image generation
📅
005 · Meeting Minutes
Turn transcripts into formal, actionable minutes
✏️
006 · Transcript Edit
Polish transcripts — fix errors, smooth sentences
Chrome Built-in AI · Prompt Design
Design Your Own Prompt
R · T · C · O
Works for almost anything.
R
Role
You are [expert / profession]
T
Task
What to do — e.g. rewrite the following paragraph
C
Constraint
How to do it — e.g. keep it professional and concise
O
Output
What you want — e.g. give me 3 versions
Module 2
Animation
with AI
MODULE 2
AI Animation
5-step workflow: Storyboard → Narration → Voiceover → Google Vids → YouTube
Module 2 · Animation
Step 1: Storyboard Slides
1
Generate outline in NLM chat
Start in the chat area first, not Slides
2
Save outline as a source (.txt)
This becomes the storyboard blueprint
3
Generate Slides with this prompt
"Strictly follow each slide's illustration suggestion, generate 5 slides"
Why 5 slides?
Google Vids works best with 4–6 images. 5 slides = 5 illustrations = a natural video length of ~60 seconds.
AI Animation Video Studio ↗
Module 2 · Animation
Step 2: Narration Script
1
Copy your storyboard outline
The same outline you generated in Step 1
2
Go to trainer website
Open the "Voice & Subtitles" section
3
Paste outline → generate narration
AI writes a natural voiceover script for each slide
4
Download the MP3
Your narration audio is ready
What you get
A natural-sounding narration script matched to each slide, read in a clear teacher voice. No recording booth needed.
Module 2 · Optional Step
Google AI Studio Voiceover
1
Open AI Studio
aistudio.google.com (personal account only)
2
Paste narration text
Copy from Step 2 output
3
Click Run → Download MP3
Higher quality voice than the trainer website
This step is optional — the trainer website MP3 from Step 2 is sufficient for most purposes.
Module 2 · Animation
Step 3: Google Vids
1
Upload your 5 storyboard images
One image per slide from your NLM presentation
2
Click Animate Image on each
Generates 4-second animated clip per image
3
Add your MP3 narration
Drag the audio file onto the timeline
4
Fix subtitles → Simplified Chinese
Select All → Google Translate → paste back
Module 2 · Try It
Full Run-Through
Complete all 5 steps and produce your animation video
✓
Step 1 · Storyboard slides generated in NLM
✓
Step 2 · Narration script written & MP3 downloaded
✓
Step 3 · Images animated in Google Vids
✓
Step 4 · Audio added to timeline
✓
Step 5 · Subtitles corrected

Module 3
ChatGPT
Image 2.0
MODULE 3
ChatGPT Image 2.0
OpenAI's latest image model — smarter than DALL·E, reads text accurately, built for teaching.
Module 3 · ChatGPT Image 2.0
Where to Find Prompts?
Two official entry points — both give you ready-made prompts to learn from
①
openai.com ↗
Switch to Classic mode → browse examples → click any image to see its exact prompt. Best for inspiration.
②
ChatGPT → "Create image" → Style cards
20+ preset styles: infographic · handwritten notes · blueprint poster · comic strip… Click any card and the prompt auto-fills. Zero prompt writing needed.
Module 3 · Teaching Applications
3 Ways to Use It for Teaching
🖼️
Poster
Quick prompt
Based on the information above, create a promotional poster
Example prompt
Design a 2026 enrolment recruitment poster for An Ning Primary School. Highlight the school's unique features. Make the tagline fun and engaging — something that makes parents excited to bring their children.
📊
Infographic
Quick prompt
Based on the information above, create an infographic
Example prompt
Create a visually rich infographic on the endangered animal Polar Bear. Search the web for its habitat, diet and unique traits. Use annotated visuals and label boxes — not paragraph sections. Style: bold graphic illustration, photorealistic central animal, diagrams, callout boxes, clean background, layered composition. Information-dense and professionally crafted.
🎞️
Comic
Quick prompt
A page of a comic book about "Kong Rong Rang Li" in the style of cute cartoon comic
Example prompt
Illustrate Li Bai's poem "Quiet Night Thoughts" as a 4-panel cartoon comic strip, easy for children to understand. Depict each line with simple plain-language captions. Full colour, ultra-high quality, 9:16 ratio.
